title: "18.10: nvidia 340 no login"

forum: Ubuntu Development Version
---

### Post by MyMurry on 2018-09-30
Hello,

since last week I can't login with the nvidia driver 340. Only if I use the recover boot option and resume, I can login.

regards,

Norbert

---

### Post by dino99 on 2018-09-30
The bios is so old :mad:  dont you get a newer one ?
First clean the system: maybe use gtkorphan/bleachbit (carefully set options)
and downgrade if you also use some third party packages (ppa-purge)
test the ram

):P

---

### Post by MyMurry on 2018-10-03
It works with lightdm instead of gdm3.

---

### Post by NiBu on 2018-10-09
I have exactly the same problem since upgrading to 18.10 one week ago. 
Installing lightdm is not a solution for me.

---

### Post by fooman on 2018-10-10
try tapping f4 and then f1,  see if you get the login screen.

Oops, sorry that should have been: ctrl+alt+ f4 (or f2, or f3) then Ctrl+alt+ f1

---

### Post by adfh on 2018-10-26
I find since I upgraded to 18.10 Cosmic, I get lots more lockups at boot time, and indeed have to switch with Ctrl+Alt+F2 ... Ctrl+Alt+F1 back again.
I also find that X is more prone to crashing with kernel panics mentioning nvidia driver as well - with the keyboard refusing to accept input, I have to log in via SSH to kill my session.

Any idea why this happens? It was working fine under 18.04.

* Gigabyte P55A-UD4 rev 1
* Intel Core i7 860 @ 2.80GHz
* Nvidia GeForce GTS 250
* Dual Dell U2413 displays (one landscape, one portrait)

... yeah, the computer's 8 years old but otherwise working fine
... Is it just that Nvidia's dropping behind on support for newer kernels with v340?

Windows 10 works just fine in dual boot.

Kernel barfs look like this:
